title: Job Vacancies at Gift Imagination
content:
Gift Imagination is a leading brand in the production, sourcing and supply of promotional items and souvenirs for organisations across sectors. We help organisations create lasting connections with their audiences through bespoke merchandise and corporate gifts that leave lasting impressions.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Logistics and Procurement Operations

Job Location: Marina, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Logistics and Operations Executive is responsible for ensuring the smooth coordination of logistics activities, order fulfillment, inventory movement, and operational execution across all projects. The role ensures that products are properly sourced, stored, dispatched, and delivered on time, while maintaining accuracy, cost efficiency, and service quality.
	The role also supports general operational coordination, vendor management, and process execution to ensure seamless end-to-end delivery of client orders.


Core Function


	Logistics coordination, inventory control, order tracking, vendor management, and operational execution support.


Key Responsibilities 
Logistics & Delivery Management:


	Coordinate all inbound and outbound logistics activities
	Ensure timely delivery of client orders and project materials
	Track shipments and provide real-time delivery updates
	Liaise with courier services, transporters, and logistics partners
	Resolve delivery issues, delays, and discrepancies promptly


Operations Execution


	Support end-to-end execution of client orders from production to delivery
	Ensure all items meet client specifications before dispatch
	Coordinate with internal teams to align production and logistics schedules
	Monitor job progress to ensure timely completion of all orders


Inventory & Stock Management:


	Maintain accurate inventory records for all products and materials
	Conduct regular stock checks and reconciliation
	Monitor stock levels and trigger replenishment when necessary
	Ensure proper storage, labeling, and organization of goods


Vendor & Supplier Coordination:


	Liaise with suppliers and vendors for timely delivery of goods and services
	Follow up on production timelines and delivery commitments
	Ensure vendors meet quality and compliance standards
	Maintain strong working relationships with logistics partners


Documentation & Reportin


	Maintain accurate records of all logistics activities and deliveries
	Prepare weekly and monthly logistics and operations reports
	Track order movement, delivery status, and performance metrics
	Ensure proper filing of delivery notes, invoices, and receipts


Process Improvement & Compliance:


	Support continuous improvement of logistics and operational processes
	Ensure compliance with internal SOPs and company standards
	Identify inefficiencies and recommend process improvements
	Assist in developing operational systems and workflow optimization


Key  Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or related field
	2–4 years of experience in logistics, operations, or supply chain role
	Strong understanding of inventory and delivery systems
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic reporting tools
	Experience working with vendors, couriers, or logistics providers is an advantage


Key Attributes & Competencies:


	Strong organizational and coordination skills
	Excellent attention to detail and accuracy
	Ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment
	Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills
	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proactive and solution-oriented mindset
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Strong sense of accountability and responsibility.


Salary
N250,000 per month.  

Application Closing Date: 11th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated curriculum Vitae to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.


2.) Sales and Business Development Executive (Procurement and Brand Merchandise)

Job Location: Marina, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking for acommercially driven and execution-focused professional to drive new business acquisition, manage strategic client relationships, and expand the corporate client portfolio.
	The ideal candidate will bring an existing network of corporate clients or business relationships and demonstrate the ability to activate and convert these networks into revenue-generating opportunities.


Job Summary


	The Sales & Business Development Executive will play a key role in driving customer acquisition, business growth, and client retention.
	The role involves actively engaging prospects, managing client relationships, attending meetings and events, and supporting the conversion of leads into long-term business opportunities.


Key Responsibilties
Business Development & Customer Relations:


	Identify, engage, and follow up on new business leads
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and partners
	Attend meetings, exhibitions, presentations, and networking events
	Represent the company professionally in all client-facing engagements
	Work closely with partners and vendors to process and fulfil client orders
	Support customer acquisition and retention strategies


Sales Execution:


	Drive sales growth and meet business development targets
	Prepare and deliver proposals and presentations to clients
	Follow up on client inquiries, quotations, and negotiations
	Convert leads into successful business deals


Client Communication:


	Manage professional email communication and correspondence
	Maintain timely follow-up on client requests and proposals
	Ensure excellent client experience from first contact to delivery.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or related fields
	3 - 4 years of experience in Sales or Business Development
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Excellent email etiquette and business correspondence skills
	Proven ability to build and maintain client relationships
	Strong presentation and negotiation skills
	Professional appearance and confident presence.
	Strong Knowledge about procurement and brand Merchandise.


Key Attributes:


	Outgoing, confident, and comfortable engaging with people
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills (written & verbal)
	Well-presented, professional, and well-dressed at all times
	Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking ability
	Proactive and solution-oriented mindset
	Highly organized and able to manage multiple tasks
	Dependable with a strong sense of responsibility
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, target-driven environment
	Customer-focused with strong networking ability.


What We Offer


	Salary: N250,000 per month.
	Competitive salary package
	HMO (Health Maintenance Organisation) coverage
	Pension contribution
	Opportunity to work with a fast-scaling premium procurement and brand solutions firm
	Exposure to high-value corporate clients and strategic sourcing networks
	Performance-driven environment with clear growth and earning potential
	High autonomy and ownership of business development outcomes.


Application Closing Date: 10th May, 2026. 

Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated curriculum Vitae to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.


3.) Operations Executive (Administration) 

Job Location: Marina, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose


	The Admin Executive is responsible for supporting and coordinating the organization’s administrative, operational, and client service functions.
	The role ensures smooth day-to-day operations through effective vendor management, accurate pricing and invoicing, inventory control, and timely execution of client orders and projects.


Key Responsibilities
Administrative & Operational Support:


	Coordinate daily administrative and operational activities
	Maintain proper documentation and filing systems (digital and physical)
	Support the development and maintenance of operational processes and SOPs


Client & Presentation Management:


	Respond to client briefs in a timely and professional manner
	Prepare PowerPoint presentations and proposal documents
	Support preparation and delivery of client samples and project materials


Procurement & Vendor Management:


	Source vendors and suppliers for promotional and gifting items
	Negotiate pricing and ensure cost efficiency
	Maintain strong vendor relationships to ensure quality and timely delivery


Pricing, Invoicing & Accounting Support:


	Analyze and compute product pricing for client orders
	Prepare and issue invoices accurately and on time
	Maintain financial records and support basic accounting activities
	Assist with expense tracking and reconciliation


Inventory & Logistics Management:


	Maintain accurate inventory records for products and packaging materials
	Monitor stock levels and provide timely reports
	Coordinate logistics and ensure timely delivery of orders and samples


Project & Order Fulfillment:


	Support execution of bulk gifting projects and campaigns
	Track orders from sourcing to final delivery
	Ensure adherence to timelines, quality standards, and client requirements


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Accounting, or related field
	3–5 years of relevant experience in administration, operations, procurement, or similar role
	Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
	Good understanding of pricing, invoicing, and basic accounting principles
	Experience in vendor sourcing and management
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	Excellent communication and coordination abilities
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.


Key Competencies:


	Administrative and operational coordination
	Vendor sourcing and negotiation
	Pricing and cost analysis
	Inventory and logistics management
	Accounting and financial record support
	Attention to detail and problem-solving.


What We Offer


	Salary: N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.
	HMO (Health Maintenance Organisation) coverage
	Pension contribution
	Opportunity to work with a fast-scaling premium procurement and brand solutions firm
	Exposure to high-value corporate clients and strategic sourcing networks
	Performance-driven environment with clear growth and earning potential
	High autonomy and ownership of business development outcomes.


Application Closing Date: 13th May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Curriculum Vitae to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Trading under a number of brands (including Regus, Spaces, HQ, Signature and No18) we have a physical network of over 3,000 locations across 1,000 towns and cities in 120 countries. Our mission is to help our millions of customers to have a great day at work, every day.

COVID-19 forced an unprecedented experiment in flexible working, which is now driving a surge in demand for our flexible workspace solutions. To meet this demand, we are rapidly accelerating the growth of our global network. To deliver our ambition we are recruiting partners to open and operate IWG locations. Our target audience are building owners, franchise operators and competitors, we will work with our partners under a variety of commercial structures demanding a dynamic and broad-based approach to business development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Customer Service Executive

Job ID: 50710
Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja
Employment Type: Full Time (On-site)

&nbsp;

Job&nbsp; Description


	This position sits at the heart of our growing global organisation and is a critical role where you will have an opportunity to shine and deliver world-class customer service. You will be part of a dynamic team with amazing career opportunities, working regular and sociable hours and with the chance to unleash your full potential.


We will expect you to:


	Give our customers and their guests a warm and friendly welcome, every day.
	Manage a range of on-site tasks to help our busy workspace run smoothly and happily.
	Above all, help your customers and colleagues have a great day at work.


About you
What really matters is that you&rsquo;ve got the right mindset. We&rsquo;ll provide everything else for you to build a fantastic career with IWG. All you need to bring is:


	Great communication skills and a love of interacting with people.
	A positive, outgoing and can-do attitude.
	The ability to take direction, learn new skills and apply them with confidence.
	The motivation to be your best every day &ndash; and the determination to be even better tomorrow.


&nbsp;What we can do for you
You are reading the right advert if you are looking for:


	A fun, challenging and rewarding career.
	Award-winning induction training and excellent ongoing learning and development.
	Fantastic promotion prospects.
	Generous, achievable incentives and sociable hours.
	Access to a comprehensive range of flexible, personalised workplace benefits that support mental, physical and financial wellbeing.

Customer Service Executive Job at International Workplace Group (IWG)




        
    

        
        International Workplace Group (IWG) is the largest provider of flexible workspace solutions in the world. Trading under a number of brands (including Regus, Spaces, HQ, Signature and No18) we have a physical network of over 3,000 locations across 1,000 towns and cities in 120 countries. Our mission is to help our millions of customers to have a great day at work, every day.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



COVID-19 forced an unprecedented experiment in flexible working, which is now driving a surge in demand for our flexible workspace solutions. To meet this demand, we are rapidly accelerating the growth of our global network. To deliver our ambition we are recruiting partners to open and operate IWG locations. Our target audience are building owners, franchise operators and competitors, we will work with our partners under a variety of commercial structures demanding a dynamic and broad-based approach to business development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive

Job ID: 50710
Job Location: Abuja
Employment Type: Full Time (On-site)

 

Job  Description


	This position sits at the heart of our growing global organisation and is a critical role where you will have an opportunity to shine and deliver world-class customer service. You will be part of a dynamic team with amazing career opportunities, working regular and sociable hours and with the chance to unleash your full potential.


We will expect you to:


	Give our customers and their guests a warm and friendly welcome, every day.
	Manage a range of on-site tasks to help our busy workspace run smoothly and happily.
	Above all, help your customers and colleagues have a great day at work.


About you
What really matters is that you’ve got the right mindset. We’ll provide everything else for you to build a fantastic career with IWG. All you need to bring is:


	Great communication skills and a love of interacting with people.
	A positive, outgoing and can-do attitude.
	The ability to take direction, learn new skills and apply them with confidence.
	The motivation to be your best every day – and the determination to be even better tomorrow.


 What we can do for you
You are reading the right advert if you are looking for:


	A fun, challenging and rewarding career.
	Award-winning induction training and excellent ongoing learning and development.
	Fantastic promotion prospects.
	Generous, achievable incentives and sociable hours.
	Access to a comprehensive range of flexible, personalised workplace benefits that support mental, physical and financial wellbeing.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Lead, Customer Support QA",
  "description": "Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors&rsquo; first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Lead, Customer Support QA

Job Location: Lagos

Role Summary


	The Quality Assurance / Assessment Manager is responsible for designing, governing, and driving service quality at scale across all customer interaction channels.
	This role ensures that every customer touchpoint; digital or physical meets defined standards for clarity, accuracy, empathy, and resolution effectiveness.
	Beyond monitoring quality, the role owns the end-to-end quality ecosystem: frameworks, calibration, audit integrity, insights generation, and continuous improvement.
	The QA Manager translates performance data into clear, actionable interventions that improve customer experience and operational outcomes.
	This role is both strategic and execution-focused setting standards while ensuring they are consistently applied across teams, regions, and channels.


Key Responsibilities
Quality Strategy &amp; Governance:


	Define and continuously refine QA frameworks and scorecards across all channels
	Ensure alignment between voice and non-voice standards for a consistent customer experience
	Maintain scoring integrity, audit readiness, and governance discipline
	Establish clear quality benchmarks tied to business outcomes (CSAT, FCR, etc.)


Team Leadership:


	Lead and develop a team of 13+ Quality Assessors
	Set performance targets, productivity benchmarks, and quality standards for the QA team
	Drive capability building through coaching, feedback sessions, and structured development plans
	Build a high-performing team with strong analytical and evaluation capabilities.


Quality Monitoring &amp; Evaluation:


	Design and oversee sampling methodologies across all channels
	Ensure balanced and representative coverage (voice, chat, social, WhatsApp, walk-in interactions)
	Validate scoring accuracy and proactively minimize evaluator bias
	Conduct periodic audits to ensure consistency and reliability of assessments.


Calibration &amp; Standardisation:


	Lead cross-functional calibration sessions with operations and training teams
	Ensure uniform interpretation of quality standards across all evaluators
	Resolve scoring discrepancies and define clear guidelines for edge cases
	Track and reduce calibration variance across teams.


Insights &amp; Continuous Improvement:


	Translate QA data into clear, actionable insights for operations and leadership


Identify root causes of performance gaps across:


	Process inefficiencies
	Training gaps
	System limitations.


Partner with operations to implement targeted interventions that improve:


	CSAT
	First Contact Resolution (FCR)
	Average Handling Time (AHT)
	Repeat contact rates.


Stakeholder Management:
Work closely with:


	Operations teams (L1 support, follow-up teams, walk-in centres)
	Training &amp; Knowledge Management
	Product and Process teams
	Act as the voice of quality in cross-functional discussions
	Present quality performance, trends, and insights to senior leadership
	Influence decision-making through data-backed recommendations.


Knowledge &amp; Process Alignment:


	Ensure QA frameworks are aligned with latest SOPs and knowledge base updates
	Identify and escalate policy gaps or inconsistencies impacting service quality
	Partner with Knowledge Management to ensure clarity, usability, and accuracy of content.


Channel Complexity Coverage:
The role ensures quality consistency across diverse interaction types:


	Voice (Inbound &amp; Outbound):&nbsp;Call handling, empathy, probing, resolution quality, compliance
	Non-Voice (Email, Chat):&nbsp;Clarity, tone, accuracy, completeness, turnaround time
	Social Media &amp; WhatsApp (including groups):&nbsp;Public tone management, responsiveness, brand risk control
	Walk-in Centres:&nbsp;In-person engagement quality, professionalism, issue resolution, experience consistency.


Key Metrics / Success Measures:


	Overall QA scores (and channel-specific performance)
	Correlation between QA scores and CSAT outcomes
	First Contact Resolution (FCR) rates
	Reduction in repeat contacts and escalations
	Calibration accuracy and variance reduction
	QA coverage and productivity levels
	Measurable improvements driven from QA insights.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies
Core Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving capability
	Deep understanding of contact centre operations and customer experience
	Proven people management and coaching skills.


Technical Competencies:


	Experience with QA tools, CRM platforms, and reporting dashboards
	Strong ability to analyze large datasets and identify actionable trends
	Familiarity with multi-channel support environments.


Behavioural Competencies


	High attention to detail and strong judgment
	Fair, consistent, and objective in evaluation
	Ability to influence stakeholders without direct authority
	Structured, data-driven, and outcome-oriented thinking.


Experience &amp; Qualifications:


	6-10+ years in contact centre operations and/or quality assurance
	Minimum 2-3 years of managing QA teams
	Proven experience in multi-channel customer support environments
	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree (relevant field preferred).


Leadership Expectations:


	Build and sustain a high-performing QA function
	Shift the organization from quality scoring to quality improvement
	Drive a culture of continuous learning and performance excellence
	Act as a critical bridge between quality, operations, and customer experience
	Lead with data, clarity, and strong operational judgment.

Lead, Customer Support QA Job at Moniepoint




        
    

        
        Moniepoint Incorporated is a global business payments and banking platform and recently became QED Investors’ first investment in Africa. We are the partner of choice for over 600,000 businesses of all sizes, powering the dreams of SMBs and providing them with equal access to the tools they need to grow and scale.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Lead, Customer Support QA

Job Location: Lagos

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Quality Assurance / Assessment Manager is responsible for designing, governing, and driving service quality at scale across all customer interaction channels.
	This role ensures that every customer touchpoint; digital or physical meets defined standards for clarity, accuracy, empathy, and resolution effectiveness.
	Beyond monitoring quality, the role owns the end-to-end quality ecosystem: frameworks, calibration, audit integrity, insights generation, and continuous improvement.
	The QA Manager translates performance data into clear, actionable interventions that improve customer experience and operational outcomes.
	This role is both strategic and execution-focused setting standards while ensuring they are consistently applied across teams, regions, and channels.


Key Responsibilities
Quality Strategy & Governance:


	Define and continuously refine QA frameworks and scorecards across all channels
	Ensure alignment between voice and non-voice standards for a consistent customer experience
	Maintain scoring integrity, audit readiness, and governance discipline
	Establish clear quality benchmarks tied to business outcomes (CSAT, FCR, etc.)


Team Leadership:


	Lead and develop a team of 13+ Quality Assessors
	Set performance targets, productivity benchmarks, and quality standards for the QA team
	Drive capability building through coaching, feedback sessions, and structured development plans
	Build a high-performing team with strong analytical and evaluation capabilities.


Quality Monitoring & Evaluation:


	Design and oversee sampling methodologies across all channels
	Ensure balanced and representative coverage (voice, chat, social, WhatsApp, walk-in interactions)
	Validate scoring accuracy and proactively minimize evaluator bias
	Conduct periodic audits to ensure consistency and reliability of assessments.


Calibration & Standardisation:


	Lead cross-functional calibration sessions with operations and training teams
	Ensure uniform interpretation of quality standards across all evaluators
	Resolve scoring discrepancies and define clear guidelines for edge cases
	Track and reduce calibration variance across teams.


Insights & Continuous Improvement:


	Translate QA data into clear, actionable insights for operations and leadership


Identify root causes of performance gaps across:


	Process inefficiencies
	Training gaps
	System limitations.


Partner with operations to implement targeted interventions that improve:


	CSAT
	First Contact Resolution (FCR)
	Average Handling Time (AHT)
	Repeat contact rates.


Stakeholder Management:
Work closely with:


	Operations teams (L1 support, follow-up teams, walk-in centres)
	Training & Knowledge Management
	Product and Process teams
	Act as the voice of quality in cross-functional discussions
	Present quality performance, trends, and insights to senior leadership
	Influence decision-making through data-backed recommendations.


Knowledge & Process Alignment:


	Ensure QA frameworks are aligned with latest SOPs and knowledge base updates
	Identify and escalate policy gaps or inconsistencies impacting service quality
	Partner with Knowledge Management to ensure clarity, usability, and accuracy of content.


Channel Complexity Coverage:
The role ensures quality consistency across diverse interaction types:


	Voice (Inbound & Outbound): Call handling, empathy, probing, resolution quality, compliance
	Non-Voice (Email, Chat): Clarity, tone, accuracy, completeness, turnaround time
	Social Media & WhatsApp (including groups): Public tone management, responsiveness, brand risk control
	Walk-in Centres: In-person engagement quality, professionalism, issue resolution, experience consistency.


Key Metrics / Success Measures:


	Overall QA scores (and channel-specific performance)
	Correlation between QA scores and CSAT outcomes
	First Contact Resolution (FCR) rates
	Reduction in repeat contacts and escalations
	Calibration accuracy and variance reduction
	QA coverage and productivity levels
	Measurable improvements driven from QA insights.


Required Skills & Competencies
Core Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving capability
	Deep understanding of contact centre operations and customer experience
	Proven people management and coaching skills.


Technical Competencies:


	Experience with QA tools, CRM platforms, and reporting dashboards
	Strong ability to analyze large datasets and identify actionable trends
	Familiarity with multi-channel support environments.


Behavioural Competencies


	High attention to detail and strong judgment
	Fair, consistent, and objective in evaluation
	Ability to influence stakeholders without direct authority
	Structured, data-driven, and outcome-oriented thinking.


Experience & Qualifications:


	6-10+ years in contact centre operations and/or quality assurance
	Minimum 2-3 years of managing QA teams
	Proven experience in multi-channel customer support environments
	Bachelor’s degree (relevant field preferred).


Leadership Expectations:


	Build and sustain a high-performing QA function
	Shift the organization from quality scoring to quality improvement
	Drive a culture of continuous learning and performance excellence
	Act as a critical bridge between quality, operations, and customer experience
	Lead with data, clarity, and strong operational judgment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at the Startup Place Limited
content:
The Startup Place Limited is a Business Consulting firm with key focus on Startup Development, Digital Marketing and Business Growth. We pull a team of passionate entrepreneurs and consultant about helping you start, grow and scale your business.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Business Development Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a high performance Business Development Manager to drive aggressive revenue growth, build strategic partnerships, and expand our diagnostic and clinical services
	This is a results-driven role designed for individuals who are hungry, disciplined, and capable of consistently delivering measurable financial outcomes


Key Responsibilities


	Generate new business leads daily through calls, visits, and outreach
	Close corporate partnerships and referral deals
	Drive internal clinic sales performance across all branches
	Build and manage a structured, high-conversion sales pipeline
	Execute corporate screenings and strategic partnerships
	Increase patient conversion into premium services (e.g., diagnostics like OCT)


Performance Targets


	Monthly Revenue Target: N20,000,000 
	Quarterly Revenue Target: N60,000,000


Daily Expectations:


	Minimum of 15 outbound calls/outreach
	At least 3 physical business visits
	Minimum of 2 new leads generated daily
	Daily report submission before 7PM
	Active pipeline management with clear deal movement


Requirements


	Proven track record in sales or business development (with measurable results)
	Strong closing and negotiation skills
	High level of discipline, consistency, and accountability
	Ability to work under pressure and meet aggressive targets
	Experience in healthcare, B2B sales, or service-based industries is an advantage.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 Monthly.

Quarterly Bonus Structure


	Bonus is awarded upon achieving the 60M naira quarterly target
	Near-target performance will be rewarded (partial bonus at management discretion)
	Compensation is structured to reward results, not activity alone.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Human Resource Assistant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)

Role Description


	This is a full-time hybrid role for a Human Resource Assistant based in Lagos. The role allows a mix of in-office and remote work.
	Responsibilities include assisting with HR tasks, providing excellent customer service, managing employee records, supporting training initiatives, and maintaining effective communication within the organization.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field is preferred
	Strong Communication and Customer Service skills
	Knowledge of Human Resources processes and procedures
	Experience with Training and development practices
	Ability to work collaboratively and maintain a professional demeanor
	Organizational and time management skills
	Proficiency in using office tools and basic HR software.


Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)

Role Description


	This is a full-time hybrid role for a Human Resources Officer, based in Lagos.
	The Human Resources Officer will manage HR activities, including implementing HR policies, developing job descriptions, fostering employee relations, and ensuring compliance with company procedures.
	Additionally, the role involves supporting recruitment efforts, onboarding processes, and providing guidance on workplace matters to maintain a productive and positive work environment.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in Human Resource Management, Business Administration, or a related field
	Strong knowledge and experience in HR Management and general Human Resources (HR) practices
	Proficiency in designing and implementing HR Policies and job description development
	Skilled in Employee Relations, with a focus on maintaining a positive and productive workplace
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Proven organizational and time management abilities
	Experience in consulting environments is a plus.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Product Design and Development Engineer",
  "description": "CornerStone Prosthetics &amp; Orthotics Limited, headquartered in Benin City, is transforming into a technology-driven rehabilitation company with proprietary clinical software, digital fabrication systems, and IoT-enabled prosthetic devices. We are building Africa&#39;s most advanced P&amp;O technology stack.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Product Design and Development Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Edo
Job type: Full-time (Hybrid)

Role Summary


	The Product Design and Development Engineer (Rehabilitation Systems) will lead the digital design, prototyping, and production of prosthetic and orthotic devices within our in-house fabrication unit.
	The role suits an engineer who is comfortable across 3D scanning, CAD, and additive manufacturing, and who enjoys developing rehabilitation products from concept to finished device.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the digital fabrication workflow from 3D scanning through CAD rectification to printed output.
	Design and iterate on prosthetic and orthotic components using parametric CAD and mesh-editing tools.
	Operate and maintain in-house 3D printing systems, including process tuning and quality control.
	Develop and document print parameters for engineering-grade polymers.
	Conduct design reviews, mechanical checks, and structured product development cycles.
	Maintain CAD documentation, version control, and design history records.
	Collaborate with clinicians and other engineering staff to refine fit, function, and patient outcomes.


Requirements
Required Skills and Qualifications:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Industrial Design, or a related field.
	Proficiency in parametric CAD software and mesh-editing tools.
	Practical experience with 3D scanning and handling of organic scan geometry.
	Hands-on experience operating industrial-grade FDM 3D printers.
	Working knowledge of design for additive manufacturing (DfAM).
	Ability to interpret mechanical drawings, tolerances, and assembly documentation.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience designing prosthetic, orthotic, or other medical devices.
	Familiarity with engineering thermoplastics and fibre-reinforced polymers.
	Exposure to mechanical testing of load-bearing components.
	Understanding of relevant medical device or prosthetic testing standards.


Salary Range
N300,000 - N400,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;careers@cornerstonepo.africa&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Product Design and Development Engineer Job at CornerStone Prosthetics & Orthotics Limited




        
    

        
        CornerStone Prosthetics & Orthotics Limited, headquartered in Benin City, is transforming into a technology-driven rehabilitation company with proprietary clinical software, digital fabrication systems, and IoT-enabled prosthetic devices. We are building Africa's most advanced P&O technology stack.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Product Design and Development Engineer

Job Location: Edo
Job type: Full-time (Hybrid)

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Product Design and Development Engineer (Rehabilitation Systems) will lead the digital design, prototyping, and production of prosthetic and orthotic devices within our in-house fabrication unit.
	The role suits an engineer who is comfortable across 3D scanning, CAD, and additive manufacturing, and who enjoys developing rehabilitation products from concept to finished device.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the digital fabrication workflow from 3D scanning through CAD rectification to printed output.
	Design and iterate on prosthetic and orthotic components using parametric CAD and mesh-editing tools.
	Operate and maintain in-house 3D printing systems, including process tuning and quality control.
	Develop and document print parameters for engineering-grade polymers.
	Conduct design reviews, mechanical checks, and structured product development cycles.
	Maintain CAD documentation, version control, and design history records.
	Collaborate with clinicians and other engineering staff to refine fit, function, and patient outcomes.


Requirements
Required Skills and Qualifications:


	Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Industrial Design, or a related field.
	Proficiency in parametric CAD software and mesh-editing tools.
	Practical experience with 3D scanning and handling of organic scan geometry.
	Hands-on experience operating industrial-grade FDM 3D printers.
	Working knowledge of design for additive manufacturing (DfAM).
	Ability to interpret mechanical drawings, tolerances, and assembly documentation.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience designing prosthetic, orthotic, or other medical devices.
	Familiarity with engineering thermoplastics and fibre-reinforced polymers.
	Exposure to mechanical testing of load-bearing components.
	Understanding of relevant medical device or prosthetic testing standards.


Salary Range
N300,000 - N400,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
